Moreover, an attention channel-squeeze module is employed for each CNN Transformer stream independently to emphasize discriminative disease related responses and suppress redundant or background driven activations. Finally, these discriminative channels are fused to achieve refined representations from the dense local and SwinV2 global correlated strengthened feature maps, respectively. The proposed Dense SwinV2 utilized a public cassava leaf disease dataset of 31000 images, comprised of five diseases, including brown streak, mosaic, green mottle, bacterial blight, and normal leaf conditions. The proposed Dense SwinV2 demonstrates a significant classification accuracy of 98.02 percent with an F1 score of 97.81 percent, outperforming well-established convolutional and transformer models. These results underline the fact that Hybrid Dense SwinV2 offers robustness and practicality in the field level diagnosis of cassava disease and real world challenges related to occlusion, noise, and complex backgrounds.

zh.title: DenseSwinV2：用于木薯叶病分类的通道注意双分支CNN Transformer学习
zh.summary: 混合DenseNet-SwinV2模型在使用本地卷积特征和全局Transformer注意力对五种疾病类型进行木薯叶病分类时实现了98.02%的准确度。这个农业AI工具可以显著改进田间作物病害诊断，减少对专家农学家的依赖。

fr.title: DenseSwinV2 : Apprentissage CNN Transformer à Double Branche Attentive aux Canaux pour la Classification des Maladies des Feuilles de Manioc
fr.summary: Un modèle hybride DenseNet-SwinV2 a atteint 98,02% de précision dans la classification des maladies des feuilles de manioc sur cinq types de maladies en utilisant à la fois les caractéristiques convolutives locales et l'attention du transformateur global. Cet outil d'IA agricole pourrait améliorer considérablement le diagnostic des maladies des cultures sur le terrain, réduisant la dépendance aux agronomes experts.

de.title: DenseSwinV2: Kanalaufmerksamkeits-Dual-Branch-CNN-Transformer-Lernen zur Klassifizierung von Kassava-Blatterkrankungen
de.summary: Ein hybrides DenseNet-SwinV2-Modell erreichte 98,02% Genauigkeit bei der Klassifizierung von Kassava-Blatterkrankungen über fünf Krankheitstypen hinweg durch die Verwendung sowohl lokaler Faltungsmerkmale als auch globaler Transformer-Aufmerksamkeit. Dieses landwirtschaftliche KI-Tool könnte die Pflanzenerkrankungsdiagnose im Feld erheblich verbessern und die Abhängigkeit von Experten-Agronomen verringern.

es.title: DenseSwinV2: Aprendizaje CNN Transformer de Rama Dual Atenta al Canal para la Clasificación de Enfermedades de Hojas de Yuca
es.summary: Un modelo híbrido DenseNet-SwinV2 logró una precisión del 98,02% en la clasificación de enfermedades de hojas de yuca en cinco tipos de enfermedades utilizando características convolucionales locales y atención de transformador global. Esta herramienta de IA agrícola podría mejorar significativamente el diagnóstico de enfermedades de cultivos en el campo, reduciendo la dependencia de agrónomos expertos.

title: Reinforcing Structured Chain-of-Thought for Video Understanding
summary: Researchers introduced Summary-Driven Reinforcement Learning (SDRL), a single-stage training approach that improves video understanding in multimodal language models without requiring costly manual annotations. The method achieved state-of-the-art results on seven video question-answering datasets by enforcing consistent reasoning and balancing exploration.
content:
arXiv:2603.25942v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) show promise in video understanding. However, their reasoning often suffers from thinking drift and weak temporal comprehension, even when enhanced by Reinforcement Learning (RL) techniques like Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO). Moreover, existing RL methods usually depend on Supervised Fine-Tuning (SFT), which requires costly Chain-of-Thought (CoT) annotation and multi-stage training, and enforces fixed reasoning paths, limiting MLLMs' ability to generalize and potentially inducing bias. To overcome these limitations, we introduce Summary-Driven Reinforcement Learning (SDRL), a novel single-stage RL framework that obviates the need for SFT by utilizing a Structured CoT format: Summarize -> Think -> Answer. SDRL introduces two self-supervised mechanisms integrated into the GRPO objective: 1) Consistency of Vision Knowledge (CVK) enforces factual grounding by reducing KL divergence among generated summaries; and 2) Dynamic Variety of Reasoning (DVR) promotes exploration by dynamically modulating thinking diversity based on group accuracy. This novel integration effectively balances alignment and exploration, supervising both the final answer and the reasoning process. Our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on seven public VideoQA datasets.

zh.title: 强化视频理解的结构化思维链
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了摘要驱动强化学习（SDRL），一种单阶段训练方法，可以改进多模态语言模型中的视频理解，无需昂贵的手动标注。该方法通过执行一致的推理和平衡探索，在七个视频问答数据集上取得了最先进的结果。

fr.title: Renforcement de la Chaîne de Pensée Structurée pour la Compréhension Vidéo
fr.summary: Des chercheurs ont introduit l'Apprentissage par Renforcement Piloté par Résumé (SDRL), une approche de formation en une seule étape qui améliore la compréhension vidéo dans les modèles de langage multimodaux sans nécessiter d'annotations manuelles coûteuses. La méthode a obtenu des résultats de pointe sur sept ensembles de données de questions-réponses vidéo en appliquant un raisonnement cohérent et en équilibrant l'exploration.

de.title: Verstärkung strukturierter Gedankenketten zum Videoverständnis
de.summary: Forscher stellten das Summary-Driven Reinforcement Learning (SDRL) vor, einen einstufigen Trainingsansatz, der das Videoverständnis in multimodalen Sprachmodellen verbessert, ohne teure manuelle Annotationen zu erfordern. Die Methode erzielte state-of-the-art-Ergebnisse bei sieben Video-Frage-Antwort-Datensätzen durch erzwungenes konsistentes Denken und ausgeglichene Erkundung.

es.title: Reforzar la Cadena de Pensamiento Estructurada para la Comprensión de Video
es.summary: Los investigadores introdujeron el Aprendizaje por Refuerzo Impulsado por Resumen (SDRL), un enfoque de entrenamiento de una sola etapa que mejora la comprensión de video en modelos de lenguaje multimodales sin requerir anotaciones manuales costosas. El método logró resultados de vanguardia en siete conjuntos de datos de preguntas y respuestas de video al aplicar razonamiento consistente y equilibrar la exploración.

title: Can Small Models Reason About Legal Documents? A Comparative Study
summary: A study of nine sub-10B parameter language models on legal benchmarks found that smaller models can match or exceed GPT-4o-mini's performance while reducing cost, latency, and privacy risks. Chain-of-thought prompting proved ineffective for legal tasks, while few-shot prompting consistently outperformed other strategies.
content:
arXiv:2603.25944v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large language models show promise for legal applications, but deploying frontier models raises concerns about cost, latency, and data privacy. We evaluate whether sub-10B parameter models can serve as practical alternatives by testing nine models across three legal benchmarks (ContractNLI, CaseHOLD, and ECtHR) using five prompting strategies (direct, chain-of-thought, few-shot, BM25 RAG, and dense RAG). Across 405 experiments with three random seeds per configuration, we find that a Mixture-of-Experts model activating only 3B parameters matches GPT-4o-mini in mean accuracy while surpassing it on legal holding identification, and that architecture and training quality matter more than raw parameter count. Our largest model (9B parameters) performs worst overall. Chain-of-thought prompting proves sharply task-dependent, improving contract entailment but degrading multiple-choice legal reasoning, while few-shot prompting emerges as the most consistently effective strategy. Comparing BM25 and dense retrieval for RAG, we find near-identical results, suggesting the bottleneck lies in the language model's utilization of retrieved context rather than retrieval quality. All experiments were conducted via cloud inference APIs at a total cost of $62, demonstrating that rigorous LLM evaluation is accessible without dedicated GPU infrastructure.

zh.title: 小模型能推理法律文件吗？一项比较研究
zh.summary: 一项针对九个参数不超过10B的语言模型在法律基准上的研究发现，较小的模型可以匹配或超过GPT-4o-mini的性能，同时降低成本、延迟和隐私风险。链式思维提示对法律任务证明无效，而少样本提示一致优于其他策略。

fr.title: Les petits modèles peuvent-ils raisonner sur les documents juridiques ? Une étude comparative
fr.summary: Une étude de neuf modèles de langage avec moins de 10B paramètres sur des benchmarks juridiques a révélé que les modèles plus petits peuvent égaler ou dépasser les performances de GPT-4o-mini tout en réduisant les coûts, la latence et les risques de confidentialité. L'incitation à la chaîne de pensée s'est avérée inefficace pour les tâches juridiques, tandis que l'apprentissage avec peu d'exemples a constamment surpassé d'autres stratégies.

de.title: Können kleine Modelle über juristische Dokumente nachdenken? Eine vergleichende Studie
de.summary: Eine Studie von neun Sprachmodellen mit weniger als 10B Parametern auf juristischen Benchmarks ergab, dass kleinere Modelle die Leistung von GPT-4o-mini erreichen oder übertreffen können und gleichzeitig Kosten, Latenz und Datenschutzrisiken reduzieren. Chain-of-Thought-Prompting erwies sich für juristische Aufgaben als unwirksam, während Few-Shot-Prompting andere Strategien durchweg überbot.

es.title: ¿Pueden los modelos pequeños razonar sobre documentos legales? Un estudio comparativo
es.summary: Un estudio de nueve modelos de lenguaje de menos de 10B parámetros en puntos de referencia legales encontró que los modelos más pequeños pueden igualar o superar el rendimiento de GPT-4o-mini mientras reducen costos, latencia y riesgos de privacidad. La indicación de cadena de pensamiento resultó ineficaz para tareas legales, mientras que la indicación de pocos ejemplos superó constantemente otras estrategias.

title: Collision-Aware Vision-Language Learning for End-to-End Driving with Multimodal Infraction Datasets
summary: Researchers developed VLAAD, a collision-aware module integrated with end-to-end autonomous driving models that improved driving scores by 14.12% in simulation and outperformed multi-billion-parameter models on real-world collision detection. This targeted approach to safety-critical behaviors addresses the primary bottleneck preventing autonomous vehicle deployment.
content:
arXiv:2603.25946v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: High infraction rates remain the primary bottleneck for end-to-end (E2E) autonomous driving, as evidenced by the low driving scores on the CARLA Leaderboard. Despite collision-related infractions being the dominant failure mode in closed-loop evaluations, collision-aware representation learning has received limited attention. To address this gap, we first develop a Video-Language-Augmented Anomaly Detector (VLAAD), leveraging a Multiple Instance Learning (MIL) formulation to obtain stable, temporally localized collision signals for proactive prediction. To transition these capabilities into closed-loop simulations, we must overcome the limitations of existing simulator datasets, which lack multimodality and are frequently restricted to simple intersection scenarios. Therefore, we introduce CARLA-Collide, a large-scale multimodal dataset capturing realistic collision events across highly diverse road networks. Trained on this diverse simulator data, VLAAD serves as a collision-aware plug-in module that can be seamlessly integrated into existing E2E driving models. By integrating our module into a pretrained TransFuser++ agent, we demonstrate a 14.12% relative increase in driving score with minimal fine-tuning. Beyond closed-loop evaluation, we further assess the generalization capability of VLAAD in an open-loop setting using real-world driving data. To support this analysis, we introduce Real-Collide, a multimodal dataset of diverse dashcam videos paired with semantically rich annotations for collision detection and prediction. On this benchmark, despite containing only 0.6B parameters, VLAAD outperforms a multi-billion-parameter vision-language model, achieving a 23.3% improvement in AUC.

zh.title: 碰撞感知视觉语言学习在端到端自动驾驶中的应用
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了VLAAD，一个碰撞感知模块集成到端到端自动驾驶模型中，在模拟中将驾驶分数提高了14.12%，并在真实世界碰撞检测中超越了数十亿参数的模型。这种针对安全关键行为的方法解决了阻止自动驾驶部署的主要瓶颈。

fr.title: Apprentissage vision-langage sensible aux collisions pour la conduite bout en bout avec des ensembles de données d'infraction multimodale
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé VLAAD, un module sensible aux collisions intégré aux modèles de conduite autonome bout en bout qui a amélioré les scores de conduite de 14,12 % en simulation et a surpassé les modèles avec des milliards de paramètres sur la détection des collisions dans le monde réel. Cette approche ciblée des comportements critiques de sécurité résout le goulot d'étranglement principal qui empêche le déploiement des véhicules autonomes.

de.title: Kollisionsbewusste Vision-Sprache-Lernverfahren für End-to-End-Fahren mit multimodalem Verstoßdatensatz
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten VLAAD, ein kollisionsbewusstes Modul, das in End-to-End-Fahrzeugautonomiemodelle integriert ist und die Fahrwerte in Simulationen um 14,12 % verbesserte und Multi-Milliarden-Parameter-Modelle bei der Kollisionserkennung in der realen Welt übertraf. Dieser gezielte Ansatz für sicherheitskritische Verhaltensweisen behebt den Hauptengpass, der die Bereitstellung autonomer Fahrzeuge verhindert.

es.title: Aprendizaje de visión-lenguaje consciente de colisiones para conducción de extremo a extremo con conjuntos de datos de infracción multimodal
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron VLAAD, un módulo consciente de colisiones integrado en modelos de conducción autónoma de extremo a extremo que mejoró los puntajes de conducción en un 14,12 % en simulación y superó a modelos con parámetros de mil millones en la detección de colisiones en el mundo real. Este enfoque específico para comportamientos críticos de seguridad aborda el cuello de botella principal que impide la implementación de vehículos autónomos.

title: When Chain-of-Thought Backfires: Evaluating Prompt Sensitivity in Medical Language Models
summary: A study of MedGemma on medical benchmarks revealed that common prompting techniques like chain-of-thought actually decrease accuracy by 5.7%, and that models are highly sensitive to answer option ordering and context truncation. These findings indicate that prompt engineering best practices from general-purpose models do not transfer to specialized medical LLMs.
content:
arXiv:2603.25960v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed in medical settings, yet their sensitivity to prompt formatting remains poorly characterized. We evaluate MedGemma (4B and 27B parameters) on MedMCQA (4,183 questions) and PubMedQA (1,000 questions) across a broad suite of robustness tests. Our experiments reveal several concerning findings. Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting decreases accuracy by 5.7% compared to direct answering. Few-shot examples degrade performance by 11.9% while increasing position bias from 0.14 to 0.47. Shuffling answer options causes the model to change predictions 59.1% of the time, with accuracy dropping up to 27.4 percentage points. Front-truncating context to 50% causes accuracy to plummet below the no-context baseline, yet back-truncation preserves 97% of full-context accuracy. We further show that cloze scoring (selecting the highest log-probability option token) achieves 51.8% (4B) and 64.5% (27B), surpassing all prompting strategies and revealing that models "know" more than their generated text shows. Permutation voting recovers 4 percentage points over single-ordering inference. These results demonstrate that prompt engineering techniques validated on general-purpose models do not transfer to domain-specific medical LLMs, and that reliable alternatives exist.

zh.title: 链式思维何时会适得其反：评估医学语言模型的提示敏感性
zh.summary: 一项对MedGemma在医学基准上的研究发现，常见的提示技术（如链式思维）实际上将准确率降低了5.7%，模型对答案选项顺序和上下文截断高度敏感。这些发现表明，通用模型的提示工程最佳实践不适用于专门的医学LLM。

fr.title: Quand la chaîne de pensée se retourne contre soi : Évaluation de la sensibilité des invites dans les modèles de langage médicaux
fr.summary: Une étude de MedGemma sur des benchmarks médicaux a révélé que les techniques d'incitation courantes comme la chaîne de pensée réduisent en réalité la précision de 5,7 %, et que les modèles sont très sensibles à l'ordre des options de réponse et à la troncature du contexte. Ces conclusions indiquent que les meilleures pratiques en matière d'ingénierie des incitations provenant de modèles à usage général ne se transfèrent pas aux LLM médicaux spécialisés.

de.title: Wenn Chain-of-Thought nach hinten losgeht: Bewertung der Prompt-Empfindlichkeit in medizinischen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Eine Studie zu MedGemma bei medizinischen Benchmarks zeigte, dass häufige Prompt-Techniken wie Chain-of-Thought die Genauigkeit tatsächlich um 5,7 % verringern und dass Modelle sehr empfindlich gegenüber der Reihenfolge der Antwortalternativen und dem Kontextabschneiden sind. Diese Erkenntnisse deuten darauf hin, dass Best Practices für Prompt-Engineering aus Modellen für allgemeine Zwecke nicht auf spezialisierte medizinische LLMs übertragen werden.

es.title: Cuando la cadena de pensamiento se vuelve en contra: Evaluación de la sensibilidad de instrucciones en modelos de lenguaje médico
es.summary: Un estudio de MedGemma en puntos de referencia médicos reveló que las técnicas de instrucción comunes como la cadena de pensamiento en realidad disminuyen la precisión en un 5,7 %, y que los modelos son altamente sensibles al orden de las opciones de respuesta y al truncamiento del contexto. Estos hallazgos indican que las mejores prácticas de ingeniería de instrucciones de modelos de propósito general no se transfieren a LLM médicos especializados.

title: Do Neurons Dream of Primitive Operators? Wake-Sleep Compression Rediscovers Schank's Event Semantics
summary: Researchers demonstrate that event-handling algorithms can automatically discover primitive operations similar to those proposed by Roger Schank through compression-based learning. On real-world commonsense data, the system discovers that mental and emotional state changes dominate events, revealing that Schank's original taxonomy was incomplete, with emotional operators like CHANGE_wants representing 20% of natural events.
content:
arXiv:2603.25975v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We show that they do. Schank's conceptual dependency theory proposed that all events decompose into primitive operations -- ATRANS, PTRANS, MTRANS, and others -- hand-coded from linguistic intuition. Can the same primitives be discovered automatically through compression pressure alone?
  We adapt DreamCoder's wake-sleep library learning to event state transformations. Given events as before/after world state pairs, our system finds operator compositions explaining each event (wake), then extracts recurring patterns as new operators optimized under Minimum Description Length (sleep). Starting from four generic primitives, it discovers operators mapping directly to Schank's: MOVE_PROP_has = ATRANS, CHANGE_location = PTRANS, SET_knows = MTRANS, SET_consumed = INGEST, plus compound operators ("mail" = ATRANS + PTRANS) and novel emotional state operators absent from Schank's taxonomy.
  We validate on synthetic events and real-world commonsense data from the ATOMIC knowledge graph. On synthetic data, discovered operators achieve Bayesian MDL within 4% of Schank's hand-coded primitives while explaining 100% of events vs. Schank's 81%. On ATOMIC, results are more dramatic: Schank's primitives explain only 10% of naturalistic events, while the discovered library explains 100%. Dominant operators are not physical-action primitives but mental and emotional state changes -- CHANGE_wants (20%), CHANGE_feels (18%), CHANGE_is (18%) -- none in Schank's original taxonomy.
  These results provide the first empirical evidence that event primitives can be derived from compression pressure, that Schank's core primitives are information-theoretically justified, and that the complete inventory is substantially richer than proposed -- with mental/emotional operators dominating in naturalistic data.

zh.title: 神经元会梦见原始算子吗？睡眠-清醒压缩重新发现Schank的事件语义
zh.summary: 研究人员展示了事件处理算法可以通过基于压缩的学习自动发现类似于Roger Schank提出的原始操作。在真实世界的常识数据上，该系统发现心理和情感状态变化主导事件，揭示Schank的原始分类法不完整，情感算子如CHANGE_wants代表自然事件的20%。

fr.title: Les neurones rêvent-ils d'opérateurs primitifs ? La compression veille-sommeil redécouvre la sémantique des événements de Schank
fr.summary: Les chercheurs démontrent que les algorithmes de traitement des événements peuvent découvrir automatiquement des opérations primitives similaires à celles proposées par Roger Schank par l'apprentissage basé sur la compression. Sur des données de sens commun du monde réel, le système découvre que les changements d'état mental et émotionnel dominent les événements, révélant que la taxonomie originale de Schank était incomplète, les opérateurs émotionnels comme CHANGE_wants représentant 20% des événements naturels.

de.title: Träumen Neuronen von primitiven Operatoren? Wach-Schlaf-Kompression entdeckt Schanks Ereignissemantik neu
de.summary: Forscher zeigen, dass Ereignisverarbeitungsalgorithmen durch kompressionsbasiertes Lernen automatisch primitive Operationen entdecken können, die Roger Schank ähnlich vorgeschlagen hat. Auf echten Allgemeinwissen-Daten stellt das System fest, dass mentale und emotionale Zustandsveränderungen Ereignisse dominieren, was zeigt, dass Schanks ursprüngliche Taxonomie unvollständig war, wobei emotionale Operatoren wie CHANGE_wants 20% der natürlichen Ereignisse ausmachen.

es.title: ¿Sueñan los neuromas con operadores primitivos? La compresión vigilia-sueño redescubre la semántica de eventos de Schank
es.summary: Los investigadores demuestran que los algoritmos de manejo de eventos pueden descubrir automáticamente operaciones primitivas similares a las propuestas por Roger Schank a través del aprendizaje basado en compresión. En datos de sentido común del mundo real, el sistema descubre que los cambios de estado mental y emocional dominan los eventos, revelando que la taxonomía original de Schank era incompleta, con operadores emocionales como CHANGE_wants representando el 20% de los eventos naturales.

title: Policy-Guided World Model Planning for Language-Conditioned Visual Navigation
summary: PiJEPA combines learned navigation policies with world model planning to improve robots' ability to follow instructions and reach visual targets. By using a policy's action distribution to guide planning, the framework achieves faster convergence and better goal-reaching accuracy than standalone policy or planning approaches.
content:
arXiv:2603.25981v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Navigating to a visually specified goal given natural language instructions remains a fundamental challenge in embodied AI. Existing approaches either rely on reactive policies that struggle with long-horizon planning, or employ world models that suffer from poor action initialization in high-dimensional spaces. We present PiJEPA, a two-stage framework that combines the strengths of learned navigation policies with latent world model planning for instruction-conditioned visual navigation. In the first stage, we finetune an Octo-based generalist policy, augmented with a frozen pretrained vision encoder (DINOv2 or V-JEPA-2), on the CAST navigation dataset to produce an informed action distribution conditioned on the current observation and language instruction. In the second stage, we use this policy-derived distribution to warm-start Model Predictive Path Integral (MPPI) planning over a separately trained JEPA world model, which predicts future latent states in the embedding space of the same frozen encoder. By initializing the MPPI sampling distribution from the policy prior rather than from an uninformed Gaussian, our planner converges faster to high-quality action sequences that reach the goal. We systematically study the effect of the vision encoder backbone, comparing DINOv2 and V-JEPA-2, across both the policy and world model components. Experiments on real-world navigation tasks demonstrate that PiJEPA significantly outperforms both standalone policy execution and uninformed world model planning, achieving improved goal-reaching accuracy and instruction-following fidelity.

zh.title: 策略引导的世界模型规划用于语言条件视觉导航
zh.summary: PiJEPA结合学习的导航策略与世界模型规划，以改进机器人遵循指令和到达视觉目标的能力。通过使用策略的动作分布来指导规划，该框架比独立的策略或规划方法实现更快的收敛和更好的目标到达准确性。

fr.title: Planification du modèle mondial guidée par la politique pour la navigation visuelle conditionnée par la langue
fr.summary: PiJEPA combine des politiques de navigation apprises avec la planification du modèle mondial pour améliorer la capacité des robots à suivre les instructions et à atteindre des cibles visuelles. En utilisant la distribution d'action d'une politique pour guider la planification, le framework atteint une convergence plus rapide et une meilleure précision d'atteinte d'objectif que les approches de politique ou de planification autonomes.

de.title: Richtlinien-geleitete Weltmodellplanung für sprachbedingte visuelle Navigation
de.summary: PiJEPA kombiniert gelernte Navigationsrichtlinien mit Weltmodellplanung, um die Fähigkeit von Robotern zu verbessern, Anweisungen zu befolgen und visuelle Ziele zu erreichen. Durch die Verwendung der Aktionsverteilung einer Richtlinie zur Anleitung der Planung erreicht das Framework schnellere Konvergenz und bessere Zielgenauigkeit als eigenständige Richtlinien- oder Planungsansätze.

es.title: Planificación del modelo mundial guiada por políticas para la navegación visual condicionada por lenguaje
es.summary: PiJEPA combina políticas de navegación aprendidas con planificación de modelos mundiales para mejorar la capacidad de los robots de seguir instrucciones y alcanzar objetivos visuales. Al usar la distribución de acciones de una política para guiar la planificación, el framework logra una convergencia más rápida y una mejor precisión de alcance de objetivos que los enfoques de política o planificación independientes.

title: Longitudinal Boundary Sharpness Coefficient Slopes Predict Time to Alzheimer's Disease Conversion in Mild Cognitive Impairment: A Survival Analysis Using the ADNI Cohort
summary: A study using structural MRI finds that tracking how quickly the gray-white matter boundary degrades over time significantly outperforms single baseline scans at predicting Alzheimer's progression in mild cognitive impairment patients. The approach could improve clinical screening and risk assessment at a fraction of the cost of PET imaging.
content:
arXiv:2603.26007v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Predicting whether someone with mild cognitive impairment (MCI) will progress to Alzheimer's disease (AD) is crucial in the early stages of neurodegeneration. This uncertainty limits enrollment in clinical trials and delays urgent treatment. The Boundary Sharpness Coefficient (BSC) measures how well-defined the gray-white matter boundary looks on structural MRI. This study measures how BSC changes over time, namely, how fast the boundary degrades each year works much better than looking at a single baseline scan for predicting MCI-to-AD conversion. This study analyzed 1,824 T1-weighted MRI scans from 450 ADNI subjects (95 converters, 355 stable; mean follow-up: 4.84 years). BSC voxel-wise maps were computed using tissue segmentation at the gray-white matter cortical ribbon. Previous studies have used CNN and RNN models that reached 96.0% accuracy for AD classification and 84.2% for MCI conversion, but those approaches disregard specific regions within the brain. This study focused specifically on the gray-white matter interface. The approach uses temporal slope features capturing boundary degradation rates, feeding them into Random Survival Forest, a non-parametric ensemble method for right-censored survival data. The Random Survival Forest trained on BSC slopes achieved a test C-index of 0.63, a 163% improvement over baseline parametric models (test C-index: 0.24). Structural MRI costs a fraction of PET imaging ($800--$1,500 vs. $5,000--$7,000) and does not require CSF collection. These temporal biomarkers could help with patient-centered safety screening as well as risk assessment.

zh.title: 纵向边界锐度系数斜率预测轻度认知障碍患者阿尔茨海默病转化时间：基于ADNI队列的生存分析
zh.summary: 一项使用结构磁共振成像的研究发现，追踪灰白质边界随时间快速退化的速度在预测轻度认知障碍患者阿尔茨海默病进展方面明显优于单一基线扫描。这种方法可以以PET成像成本的一小部分改进临床筛查和风险评估。

fr.title: Les pentes du coefficient de netteté des frontières longitudinales prédisent le délai de conversion de la maladie d'Alzheimer chez les patients atteints de déficience cognitive légère : Une analyse de survie utilisant la cohorte ADNI
fr.summary: Une étude utilisant l'IRM structurelle constate que le suivi de la rapidité avec laquelle la frontière matière grise-blanche se dégrade au fil du temps surpasse considérablement les analyses de base uniques pour prédire la progression d'Alzheimer chez les patients atteints de déficience cognitive légère. L'approche pourrait améliorer le dépistage clinique et l'évaluation des risques à une fraction du coût de l'imagerie TEP.

de.title: Longitudinale Steigungen des Grenzschärfe-Koeffizients sagen die Zeit bis zur Alzheimer-Krankheits-Umwandlung bei leichter kognitiver Beeinträchtigung voraus: Eine Überlebensanalyse unter Verwendung der ADNI-Kohorte
de.summary: Eine Studie mit struktureller MRT stellt fest, dass die Verfolgung, wie schnell die Grau-Weiß-Materie-Grenze im Laufe der Zeit degeneriert, bei der Vorhersage des Alzheimer-Fortschritts bei Patienten mit leichter kognitiver Beeinträchtigung erheblich besser ist als einzelne Ausgangsscans. Der Ansatz könnte das klinische Screening und die Risikobewertung zu einem Bruchteil der Kosten der PET-Bildgebung verbessern.

es.title: Las pendientes del coeficiente de nitidez de límite longitudinal predicen el tiempo de conversión a la enfermedad de Alzheimer en el deterioro cognitivo leve: Un análisis de supervivencia utilizando la cohorte ADNI
es.summary: Un estudio que utiliza resonancia magnética estructural encuentra que el seguimiento de la rapidez con que se degrada el límite de materia gris-blanca con el tiempo supera significativamente los escaneos de referencia únicos para predecir la progresión del Alzheimer en pacientes con deterioro cognitivo leve. El enfoque podría mejorar el cribado clínico y la evaluación de riesgos a una fracción del costo de la imagen PET.

title: FairLLaVA: Fairness-Aware Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning for Large Vision-Language Assistants
summary: FairLLaVA addresses demographic bias in multimodal AI systems by reducing disparities in visual medical imaging tasks without compromising overall performance. The parameter-efficient method demonstrates consistent improvements in fairness and clinical accuracy across applications like chest X-ray analysis and skin lesion assessment.
content:
arXiv:2603.26008v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: While powerful in image-conditioned generation, multimodal large language models (MLLMs) can display uneven performance across demographic groups, highlighting fairness risks. In safety-critical clinical settings, such disparities risk producing unequal diagnostic narratives and eroding trust in AI-assisted decision-making. While fairness has been studied extensively in vision-only and language-only models, its impact on MLLMs remains largely underexplored. To address these biases, we introduce FairLLaVA, a parameter-efficient fine-tuning method that mitigates group disparities in visual instruction tuning without compromising overall performance. By minimizing the mutual information between target attributes, FairLLaVA regularizes the model's representations to be demographic-invariant. The method can be incorporated as a lightweight plug-in, maintaining efficiency with low-rank adapter fine-tuning, and provides an architecture-agnostic approach to fair visual instruction following. Extensive experiments on large-scale chest radiology report generation and dermoscopy visual question answering benchmarks show that FairLLaVA consistently reduces inter-group disparities while improving both equity-scaled clinical performance and natural language generation quality across diverse medical imaging modalities. Code can be accessed at https://github.com/bhosalems/FairLLaVA.

zh.title: FairLLaVA：大型视觉语言助手的公平感知参数高效微调
zh.summary: FairLLaVA通过减少视觉医学影像任务中的差异来解决多模态AI系统中的人口统计偏见，同时不损害整体性能。这种参数高效的方法在胸部X光分析和皮肤病变评估等应用中展示了一致的公平性和临床准确性改进。

fr.title: FairLLaVA : Fine-tuning paramètre-efficace et conscient de l'équité pour les grands assistants vision-langage
fr.summary: FairLLaVA aborde les biais démographiques dans les systèmes d'IA multimodaux en réduisant les disparités dans les tâches d'imagerie médicale visuelle sans compromettre les performances globales. La méthode efficace en paramètres démontre des améliorations cohérentes en matière d'équité et de précision clinique dans des applications comme l'analyse des radiographies thoraciques et l'évaluation des lésions cutanées.

de.title: FairLLaVA: Fairness-bewusstes parametereffizientes Fine-Tuning für große Vision-Sprache-Assistenten
de.summary: FairLLaVA behebt demografische Vorurteile in multimodalen KI-Systemen, indem es Disparitäten bei visuellen medizinischen Bildgebungsaufgaben reduziert, ohne die Gesamtleistung zu beeinträchtigen. Die parametereffizienz Methode zeigt konsistente Verbesserungen bei Fairness und klinischer Genauigkeit in Anwendungen wie Bruströntgen-Analyse und Hautläsionsbewertung.

es.title: FairLLaVA: Ajuste fino eficiente en parámetros consciente de la equidad para grandes asistentes de visión-lenguaje
es.summary: FairLLaVA aborda el sesgo demográfico en sistemas de IA multimodales al reducir disparidades en tareas de imágenes médicas visuales sin comprometer el rendimiento general. El método eficiente en parámetros demuestra mejoras consistentes en equidad y precisión clínica en aplicaciones como análisis de radiografías de tórax y evaluación de lesiones cutáneas.

title: VLAgeBench: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models for Zero-Shot Human Age Estimation
summary: A comprehensive benchmark shows that general-purpose large vision-language models like GPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet can perform competitively at facial age estimation without fine-tuning. Findings reveal both the promise of LVLMs for biometric applications and significant fairness challenges across demographic groups.
content:
arXiv:2603.26015v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Human age estimation from facial images represents a challenging computer vision task with significant applications in biometrics, healthcare, and human-computer interaction. While traditional deep learning approaches require extensive labeled datasets and domain-specific training, recent advances in large vision-language models (LVLMs) offer the potential for zero-shot age estimation. This study presents a comprehensive zero-shot evaluation of state-of-the-art Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) for facial age estimation, a task traditionally dominated by domain-specific convolutional networks and supervised learning. We assess the performance of GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 Sonnet, and LLaMA 3.2 Vision on two benchmark datasets, UTKFace and FG-NET, without any fine-tuning or task-specific adaptation. Using eight evaluation metrics, including MAE, MSE, RMSE, MAPE, MBE, $R^2$, CCC, and $\pm$5-year accuracy, we demonstrate that general-purpose LVLMs can deliver competitive performance in zero-shot settings. Our findings highlight the emergent capabilities of LVLMs for accurate biometric age estimation and position these models as promising tools for real-world applications. Additionally, we highlight performance disparities linked to image quality and demographic subgroups, underscoring the need for fairness-aware multimodal inference. This work introduces a reproducible benchmark and positions LVLMs as promising tools for real-world applications in forensic science, healthcare monitoring, and human-computer interaction. The benchmark focuses on strict zero-shot inference without fine-tuning and highlights remaining challenges related to prompt sensitivity, interpretability, computational cost, and demographic fairness.

zh.title: VLAgeBench：大型视觉语言模型零样本人类年龄估计基准
zh.summary: 一项综合基准显示，GPT-4o和Claude 3.5 Sonnet等通用大型视觉语言模型可以在没有微调的情况下在面部年龄估计方面具有竞争力。研究结果揭示了大型视觉语言模型在生物识别应用中的前景以及跨人口统计群体的显著公平性挑战。

fr.title: VLAgeBench : Benchmark des grands modèles vision-langage pour l'estimation d'âge humain sans apprentissage
fr.summary: Un benchmark complet montre que les grands modèles vision-langage à usage général comme GPT-4o et Claude 3.5 Sonnet peuvent rivaliser pour l'estimation d'âge facial sans fine-tuning. Les résultats révèlent à la fois la promesse des LVLMs pour les applications biométriques et des défis importants en matière d'équité entre les groupes démographiques.

de.title: VLAgeBench: Bewertung großer Vision-Sprache-Modelle für die Zero-Shot-Schätzung des menschlichen Alters
de.summary: Eine umfassende Benchmark zeigt, dass allgemeine große Vision-Sprache-Modelle wie GPT-4o und Claude 3.5 Sonnet ohne Fine-Tuning bei der Gesichtsaltersschätzung konkurrenzfähig sind. Die Ergebnisse zeigen sowohl das Potenzial von GVSMs für biometrische Anwendungen als auch erhebliche Fairness-Herausforderungen über demografische Gruppen hinweg.

es.title: VLAgeBench: Evaluación de grandes modelos de visión-lenguaje para estimación de edad humana sin aprendizaje previo
es.summary: Un benchmark integral muestra que los grandes modelos vision-lenguaje de propósito general como GPT-4o y Claude 3.5 Sonnet pueden competir en la estimación de edad facial sin ajuste fino. Los hallazgos revelan tanto la promesa de los modelos vision-lenguaje para aplicaciones biométricas como desafíos significativos de equidad entre grupos demográficos.

title: Unlabeled Cross-Center Automatic Analysis for TAAD: An Integrated Framework from Segmentation to Clinical Features
summary: Researchers developed an unsupervised learning framework that automatically extracts clinically actionable features for Type A aortic dissection evaluation across hospital systems without requiring labeled data. Validation from cardiovascular surgeons confirms the framework provides meaningful assistance for preoperative assessment.
content:
arXiv:2603.26019v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Type A Aortic Dissection (TAAD) is a life-threatening cardiovascular emergency that demands rapid and precise preoperative evaluation. While key anatomical and pathological features are decisive for surgical planning, current research focuses predominantly on improving segmentation accuracy, leaving the reliable, quantitative extraction of clinically actionable features largely under-explored. Furthermore, constructing comprehensive TAAD datasets requires labor-intensive, expert level pixel-wise annotations, which is impractical for most clinical institutions. Due to significant domain shift, models trained on a single center dataset also suffer from severe performance degradation during cross-institutional deployment. This study addresses a clinically critical challenge: the accurate extraction of key TAAD clinical features during cross-institutional deployment in the total absence of target-domain annotations. To this end, we propose an unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA)-driven framework for the automated extraction of TAAD clinical features. The framework leverages limited source-domain labels while effectively adapting to unlabeled data from target domains. Tailored for real-world emergency workflows, our framework aims to achieve stable cross-institutional multi-class segmentation, reliable and quantifiable clinical feature extraction, and practical deployability independent of high-cost annotations. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our method significantly improves cross-domain segmentation performance compared to existing state-of-the-art approaches. More importantly, a reader study involving multiple cardiovascular surgeons confirms that the automatically extracted clinical features provide meaningful assistance for preoperative assessment, highlighting the practical utility of the proposed end-to-end segmentation-to-feature pipeline.

zh.title: 无标签跨中心自动分析TAAD框架：从分割到临床特征的集成框架
zh.summary: 研究人员开发了一个无监督学习框架，可自动为跨医院系统的A型主动脉夹层评估提取临床可行的特征，无需标记数据。心血管外科医生的验证证实该框架为术前评估提供了有意义的帮助。

fr.title: Analyse automatique sans étiquette multi-centres pour TAAD : Un cadre intégré de la segmentation aux caractéristiques cliniques
fr.summary: Les chercheurs ont développé un cadre d'apprentissage non supervisé qui extrait automatiquement les caractéristiques cliniquement exploitables pour l'évaluation de la dissection aortique de type A dans les systèmes hospitaliers sans nécessiter de données étiquetées. La validation par des chirurgiens cardiovasculaires confirme que le cadre fournit une assistance significative pour l'évaluation préopératoire.

de.title: Automatische Analyse ohne Kennzeichnung zwischen Zentren für TAAD: Ein integriertes Rahmenwerk von Segmentierung zu klinischen Merkmalen
de.summary: Forscher entwickelten ein unüberwachtes Lernrahmenwerk, das automatisch klinisch verwertbare Merkmale für die Bewertung der Typ-A-Aortendissektion über Krankenhaussysteme hinweg ohne gekennzeichnete Daten extrahiert. Die Validierung durch Herzchirurgen bestätigt, dass das Rahmenwerk aussagekräftige Unterstützung für die präoperative Bewertung bietet.

es.title: Análisis automático sin etiqueta entre centros para TAAD: Un marco integrado de segmentación a características clínicas
es.summary: Los investigadores desarrollaron un marco de aprendizaje no supervisado que extrae automáticamente características clínicamente procesables para la evaluación de la disección aórtica tipo A en sistemas hospitalarios sin requerir datos etiquetados. La validación de cirujanos cardiovasculares confirma que el marco proporciona asistencia significativa para la evaluación preoperatoria.

title: Designing Fatigue-Aware VR Interfaces via Biomechanical Models
summary: A reinforcement learning framework uses biomechanical fatigue models to optimize virtual reality interface layouts for reduced arm fatigue during mid-air interaction. Experiments confirm that fatigue trends from simulation align with human data and that RL-optimized layouts produce significantly lower perceived fatigue.
content:
arXiv:2603.26031v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Prolonged mid-air interaction in virtual reality (VR) causes arm fatigue and discomfort, negatively affecting user experience. Incorporating ergonomic considerations into VR user interface (UI) design typically requires extensive human-in-the-loop evaluation. Although biomechanical models have been used to simulate human behavior in HCI tasks, their application as surrogate users for ergonomic VR UI design remains underexplored. We propose a hierarchical reinforcement learning framework that leverages biomechanical user models to evaluate and optimize VR interfaces for mid-air interaction. A motion agent is trained to perform button-press tasks in VR under sequential conditions, using realistic movement strategies and estimating muscle-level effort via a validated three-compartment control with recovery (3CC-r) fatigue model. The simulated fatigue output serves as feedback for a UI agent that optimizes UI element layout via reinforcement learning (RL) to minimize fatigue. We compare the RL-optimized layout against a manually-designed centered baseline and a Bayesian optimized baseline. Results show that fatigue trends from the biomechanical model align with human user data. Moreover, the RL-optimized layout using simulated fatigue feedback produced significantly lower perceived fatigue in a follow-up human study. We further demonstrate the framework's extensibility via a simulated case study on longer sequential tasks with non-uniform interaction frequencies. To our knowledge, this is the first work using simulated biomechanical muscle fatigue as a direct optimization signal for VR UI layout design. Our findings highlight the potential of biomechanical user models as effective surrogate tools for ergonomic VR interface design, enabling efficient early-stage iteration with less reliance on extensive human participation.

zh.title: 通过生物力学模型设计疲劳感知VR界面
zh.summary: 一个强化学习框架利用生物力学疲劳模型来优化虚拟现实界面布局，以减少中空交互过程中的手臂疲劳。实验证实了模拟中的疲劳趋势与人类数据一致，RL优化的布局显著降低了感知疲劳。

fr.title: Conception d'Interfaces VR Conscientes de la Fatigue via des Modèles Biomécaniques
fr.summary: Un cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement utilise des modèles de fatigue biomécaniques pour optimiser les dispositions des interfaces de réalité virtuelle afin de réduire la fatigue des bras lors de l'interaction en l'air. Les expériences confirment que les tendances de fatigue de la simulation s'alignent avec les données humaines et que les dispositions optimisées par RL produisent une fatigue perçue nettement inférieure.

de.title: Gestaltung von Ermüdungs-bewussten VR-Schnittstellen durch Biomechanische Modelle
de.summary: Ein Verstärkungslernrahmen nutzt biomechanische Ermüdungsmodelle, um Layouts virtueller Realitätsschnittstellen zu optimieren und die Armermüdung während der Interaktion in der Luft zu reduzieren. Experimente bestätigen, dass Ermüdungstrends aus der Simulation mit Menschendaten übereinstimmen und dass RL-optimierte Layouts eine deutlich geringere wahrgenommene Ermüdung erzeugen.

es.title: Diseño de Interfaces VR Conscientes de la Fatiga mediante Modelos Biomecánicos
es.summary: Un marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo utiliza modelos de fatiga biomecánica para optimizar diseños de interfaces de realidad virtual para reducir la fatiga del brazo durante la interacción en el aire. Los experimentos confirman que las tendencias de fatiga de la simulación se alinean con los datos humanos y que los diseños optimizados por RL producen una fatiga percibida significativamente menor.

title: H-Node Attack and Defense in Large Language Models
summary: Researchers identify specific hidden-state dimensions in LLMs that encode hallucination patterns and develop both white-box attacks and adaptive defenses operating at this mechanistic level. The defense reduces hallucination while preserving general model performance with surgical impact on reasoning capabilities.
content:
arXiv:2603.26045v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: We present H-Node Adversarial Noise Cancellation (H-Node ANC), a mechanistic framework that identifies, exploits, and defends hallucination representations in transformer-based large language models (LLMs) at the level of individual hidden-state dimensions. A logistic regression probe trained on last-token hidden states localizes hallucination signal to a small set of high-variance dimensions -- termed Hallucination Nodes (H-Nodes) -- with probe AUC reaching 0.90 across four architectures. A white-box adversarial attack amplifies these dimensions at inference time via a real-time forward hook, achieving a selectivity of 3.02x with less than 10% visibility to the defender. Adaptive ANC defense suppresses H-Node excess in-pass using confidence-weighted cancellation, reducing grounded activation drift by 33-42% over static cancellation. A dynamic iterative extension that re-ranks cancellation targets across successive passes recovers up to 0.69 robustness from a single-pass baseline of 8%. All contributions are validated on OPT-125M, Phi-3-mini-4k-instruct, LLaMA-3-8B-Instruct, and Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.3 (125M-8B parameters). Perplexity impact is surgical (<5%) and MMLU degradation is at most 3%, confirming that the defense does not impair general reasoning capability.

zh.title: 大型语言模型中的H节点攻击和防御
zh.summary: 研究人员识别了LLMs中编码幻觉模式的特定隐藏状态维度，并开发了在这个机制水平上运作的白盒攻击和自适应防御。防御减少了幻觉，同时保持了一般模型性能，对推理能力产生了手术般精准的影响。

fr.title: Attaque H-Node et Défense dans les Grands Modèles de Langage
fr.summary: Les chercheurs identifient des dimensions d'état caché spécifiques dans les LLM qui codent les motifs d'hallucination et développent à la fois des attaques en boîte blanche et des défenses adaptatives fonctionnant à ce niveau mécanistique. La défense réduit les hallucinations tout en préservant les performances générales du modèle avec un impact chirurgical sur les capacités de raisonnement.

de.title: H-Knoten-Angriff und -Verteidigung in Großen Sprachmodellen
de.summary: Forscher identifizieren spezifische verborgene Zustandsdimensionen in LLMs, die Halluzinationsmuster kodieren, und entwickeln sowohl White-Box-Angriffe als auch adaptive Verteidigungsmechanismen auf dieser mechanistischen Ebene. Die Verteidigung reduziert Halluzinationen und bewahrt gleichzeitig die allgemeine Modellleistung mit chirurgischer Präzision der Auswirkungen auf Denkfähigkeiten.

es.title: Ataque y Defensa de H-Nodo en Grandes Modelos de Lenguaje
es.summary: Los investigadores identifican dimensiones de estado oculto específicas en LLMs que codifican patrones de alucinación y desarrollan tanto ataques de caja blanca como defensas adaptativas que operan a este nivel mecanicista. La defensa reduce las alucinaciones mientras preserva el rendimiento general del modelo con un impacto quirúrgico en las capacidades de razonamiento.

title: Seeing Like Radiologists: Context- and Gaze-Guided Vision-Language Pretraining for Chest X-rays
summary: CoGaze introduces a vision-language pretraining framework for medical imaging that incorporates clinical context and radiologists' gaze patterns to improve diagnostic reasoning. The model outperforms existing approaches on report generation, disease classification, and image-text retrieval for chest X-rays.
content:
arXiv:2603.26049v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Despite recent advances in medical vision-language pretraining, existing models still struggle to capture the diagnostic workflow: radiographs are typically treated as context-agnostic images, while radiologists' gaze -- a crucial cue for visual reasoning -- remains largely underexplored by existing methods. These limitations hinder the modeling of disease-specific patterns and weaken cross-modal alignment. To bridge this gap, we introduce CoGaze, a Context- and Gaze-guided vision-language pretraining framework for chest X-rays. We first propose a context-infused vision encoder that models how radiologists integrate clinical context -- including patient history, symptoms, and diagnostic intent -- to guide diagnostic reasoning. We then present a multi-level supervision paradigm that (1) enforces intra- and inter-modal semantic alignment through hybrid-positive contrastive learning, (2) injects diagnostic priors via disease-aware cross-modal representation learning, and (3) leverages radiologists' gaze as probabilistic priors to guide attention toward diagnostically salient regions. Extensive experiments demonstrate that CoGaze consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods across diverse tasks, achieving up to +2.0% CheXbertF1 and +1.2% BLEU2 for free-text and structured report generation, +23.2% AUROC for zero-shot classification, and +12.2% Precision@1 for image-text retrieval. Code is available at https://github.com/mk-runner/CoGaze.

zh.title: 像放射科医生一样观察：用于胸部X光的上下文和眼动引导的视觉-语言预训练
zh.summary: CoGaze引入了一个医学成像的视觉-语言预训练框架，整合了临床背景和放射科医生的眼动模式，以改进诊断推理。该模型在胸部X光的报告生成、疾病分类和图像-文本检索方面超越了现有方法。

fr.title: Voir comme les Radiologues : Préentraînement Vision-Langage Guidé par le Contexte et le Regard pour les Radiographies Thoraciques
fr.summary: CoGaze introduit un cadre de préentraînement vision-langage pour l'imagerie médicale qui intègre le contexte clinique et les modèles de regard des radiologues pour améliorer le raisonnement diagnostique. Le modèle surpasse les approches existantes en génération de rapports, classification des maladies et récupération d'images-texte pour les radiographies thoraciques.

de.title: Wie Radiologen Sehen: Kontext- und Blick-geführtes Vision-Language-Vortraining für Bruströntgenaufnahmen
de.summary: CoGaze führt ein Vision-Language-Vortraining-Framework für medizinische Bildgebung ein, das klinischen Kontext und die Blickmuster von Radiologen integriert, um diagnostisches Denken zu verbessern. Das Modell übertrifft bestehende Ansätze bei der Berichtsgenerierung, Krankheitsklassifizierung und Bild-Text-Abruf für Bruströntgenaufnahmen.

es.title: Ver Como Radiólogos: Preentrenamiento Vision-Lenguaje Guiado por Contexto y Mirada para Radiografías de Pecho
es.summary: CoGaze introduce un marco de preentrenamiento vision-lenguaje para imágenes médicas que incorpora contexto clínico y patrones de mirada de radiólogos para mejorar el razonamiento diagnóstico. El modelo supera enfoques existentes en generación de reportes, clasificación de enfermedades y recuperación de imagen-texto para radiografías de pecho.

title: Bridging Pixels and Words: Mask-Aware Local Semantic Fusion for Multimodal Media Verification
summary: MaLSF addresses multimodal misinformation by shifting from global fusion to active bidirectional verification that identifies local semantic conflicts between images and text. Using mask-label pairs as semantic anchors, the framework achieves state-of-the-art results on fake news detection tasks.
content:
arXiv:2603.26052v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: As multimodal misinformation becomes more sophisticated, its detection and grounding are crucial. However, current multimodal verification methods, relying on passive holistic fusion, struggle with sophisticated misinformation. Due to 'feature dilution,' global alignments tend to average out subtle local semantic inconsistencies, effectively masking the very conflicts they are designed to find. We introduce MaLSF (Mask-aware Local Semantic Fusion), a novel framework that shifts the paradigm to active, bidirectional verification, mimicking human cognitive cross-referencing. MaLSF utilizes mask-label pairs as semantic anchors to bridge pixels and words. Its core mechanism features two innovations: 1) a Bidirectional Cross-modal Verification (BCV) module that acts as an interrogator, using parallel query streams (Text-as-Query and Image-as-Query) to explicitly pinpoint conflicts; and 2) a Hierarchical Semantic Aggregation (HSA) module that intelligently aggregates these multi-granularity conflict signals for task-specific reasoning. In addition, to extract fine-grained mask-label pairs, we introduce a set of diverse mask-label pair extraction parsers. MaLSF achieves state-of-the-art performance on both the DGM4 and multimodal fake news detection tasks. Extensive ablation studies and visualization results further verify its effectiveness and interpretability.

zh.title: 连接像素和文字：用于多模态媒体验证的掩码感知局部语义融合
zh.summary: MaLSF通过从全局融合转向主动双向验证来解决多模态错误信息问题，识别图像和文本之间的局部语义冲突。使用掩码标签对作为语义锚点，该框架在虚假新闻检测任务中取得了最先进的结果。

fr.title: Fusion sémantique locale sensible aux masques pour la vérification de médias multimodaux
fr.summary: MaLSF aborde la désinformation multimodale en passant de la fusion globale à une vérification bidirectionnelle active qui identifie les conflits sémantiques locaux entre les images et le texte. En utilisant des paires masque-étiquette comme ancres sémantiques, le cadre atteint des résultats de pointe dans les tâches de détection de fausses nouvelles.

de.title: Maskenorientierte lokale semantische Fusion zur multimodalen Medienverifizierung
de.summary: MaLSF behandelt multimodale Desinformation durch den Übergang von globaler Fusion zu aktiver bidirektionaler Verifizierung, die lokale semantische Konflikte zwischen Bildern und Text identifiziert. Mit Masken-Label-Paaren als semantische Anker erreicht das Framework hochmoderne Ergebnisse bei der Erkennung von Falschnachrichten.

es.title: Fusión semántica local consciente de máscaras para verificación multimedia
es.summary: MaLSF aborda la desinformación multimodal cambiando de la fusión global a la verificación bidireccional activa que identifica conflictos semánticos locales entre imágenes y texto. Utilizando pares máscara-etiqueta como anclajes semánticos, el marco logra resultados de última generación en tareas de detección de noticias falsas.

title: MuDD: A Multimodal Deception Detection Dataset and GSR-Guided Progressive Distillation for Non-Contact Deception Detection
summary: Researchers introduce MuDD, a large-scale dataset with video, audio, physiological signals, and personality data for deception detection, alongside a cross-modal knowledge distillation framework leveraging galvanic skin response to improve non-contact deception detection. The approach adaptively transfers knowledge across modalities with different reliability levels.
content:
arXiv:2603.26064v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Non-contact automatic deception detection remains challenging because visual and auditory deception cues often lack stable cross-subject patterns. In contrast, galvanic skin response (GSR) provides more reliable physiological cues and has been widely used in contact-based deception detection. In this work, we leverage stable deception-related knowledge in GSR to guide representation learning in non-contact modalities through cross-modal knowledge distillation. A key obstacle, however, is the lack of a suitable dataset for this setting. To address this, we introduce MuDD, a large-scale Multimodal Deception Detection dataset containing recordings from 130 participants over 690 minutes. In addition to video, audio, and GSR, MuDD also provides Photoplethysmography, heart rate, and personality traits, supporting broader scientific studies of deception. Based on this dataset, we propose GSR-guided Progressive Distillation (GPD), a cross-modal distillation framework for mitigating the negative transfer caused by the large modality mismatch between GSR and non-contact signals. The core innovation of GPD is the integration of progressive feature-level and digit-level distillation with dynamic routing, which allows the model to adaptively determine how teacher knowledge should be transferred during training, leading to more stable cross-modal knowledge transfer. Extensive experiments and visualizations show that GPD outperforms existing methods and achieves state-of-the-art performance on both deception detection and concealed-digit identification.

zh.title: MuDD：多模态欺骗检测数据集和用于非接触欺骗检测的GSR引导渐进蒸馏
zh.summary: 研究人员引入了MuDD，一个包含视频、音频、生理信号和人格数据的大规模数据集用于欺骗检测，以及一个利用皮肤电反应的跨模态知识蒸馏框架来改进非接触欺骗检测。该方法跨模态自适应地转移不同可靠性级别的知识。

fr.title: MuDD : Ensemble de données de détection de tromperie multimodale et distillation progressive guidée par GSR pour la détection sans contact
fr.summary: Les chercheurs introduisent MuDD, un ensemble de données à grande échelle avec vidéo, audio, signaux physiologiques et données de personnalité pour la détection de tromperie, ainsi qu'un cadre de distillation des connaissances inter-modales exploitant la réaction galvanique de la peau pour améliorer la détection de tromperie sans contact. L'approche transfère de manière adaptative les connaissances entre les modalités avec différents niveaux de fiabilité.

de.title: MuDD: Multimodales Betrugerkennungsdatensatz und GSR-gesteuerte progressive Destillation zur kontaktlosen Betrugserkennung
de.summary: Forscher stellen MuDD vor, einen großen Datensatz mit Video, Audio, physiologischen Signalen und Persönlichkeitsdaten zur Betrugserkennung, zusammen mit einem Cross-Modal-Wissensdestillations-Framework, das die galvanische Hautreaktion nutzt, um die kontaktlose Betrugserkennung zu verbessern. Der Ansatz überträgt adaptiv Wissen über Modalitäten mit unterschiedlichen Zuverlässigkeitsstufen.

es.title: MuDD: Conjunto de datos de detección multimodal de engaños y destilación progresiva guiada por GSR para detección sin contacto
es.summary: Los investigadores presentan MuDD, un conjunto de datos a gran escala con video, audio, señales fisiológicas y datos de personalidad para la detección de engaños, junto con un marco de destilación de conocimiento multimodal que aprovecha la respuesta galvánica de la piel para mejorar la detección de engaños sin contacto. El enfoque transfiere adaptativamente el conocimiento entre modalidades con diferentes niveles de confiabilidad.

title: R-PGA: Robust Physical Adversarial Camouflage Generation via Relightable 3D Gaussian Splatting
summary: R-PGA develops a realistic adversarial attack framework for autonomous driving systems that generates camouflage textures robust to varying lighting, viewing angles, and weather conditions. Using photo-realistic 3D rendering and worst-case scenario mining, the attack significantly outperforms prior methods in robustness.
content:
arXiv:2603.26067v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Physical adversarial camouflage poses a severe security threat to autonomous driving systems by mapping adversarial textures onto 3D objects. Nevertheless, current methods remain brittle in complex dynamic scenarios, failing to generalize across diverse geometric (e.g., viewing configurations) and radiometric (e.g., dynamic illumination, atmospheric scattering) variations. We attribute this deficiency to two fundamental limitations in simulation and optimization. First, the reliance on coarse, oversimplified simulations (e.g., via CARLA) induces a significant domain gap, confining optimization to a biased feature space. Second, standard strategies targeting average performance result in a rugged loss landscape, leaving the camouflage vulnerable to configuration shifts.To bridge these gaps, we propose the Relightable Physical 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) based Attack framework (R-PGA). Technically, to address the simulation fidelity issue, we leverage 3DGS to ensure photo-realistic reconstruction and augment it with physically disentangled attributes to decouple intrinsic material from lighting. Furthermore, we design a hybrid rendering pipeline that leverages precise Relightable 3DGS for foreground rendering, while employing a pre-trained image translation model to synthesize plausible relighted backgrounds that align with the relighted foreground.To address the optimization robustness issue, we propose the Hard Physical Configuration Mining (HPCM) module, designed to actively mine worst-case physical configurations and suppress their corresponding loss peaks. This strategy not only diminishes the overall loss magnitude but also effectively flattens the rugged loss landscape, ensuring consistent adversarial effectiveness and robustness across varying physical configurations.

zh.title: R-PGA：通过可重新照明的3D高斯喷溅生成鲁棒物理对抗伪装
zh.summary: R-PGA为自动驾驶系统开发了一个逼真的对抗攻击框架，生成对不同光照、观看角度和天气条件具有鲁棒性的伪装纹理。使用照片级逼真的3D渲染和最坏情况场景挖掘，该攻击显著超越了先前方法的鲁棒性。

fr.title: R-PGA : Génération de camouflage adversaire physique robuste via Gaussian Splatting 3D relighting
fr.summary: R-PGA développe un cadre d'attaque adversaire réaliste pour les systèmes de conduite autonome qui génère des textures de camouflage robustes à l'éclairage variable, aux angles de vue et aux conditions météorologiques. Utilisant un rendu 3D photo-réaliste et l'exploration de scénarios du pire cas, l'attaque surpasse considérablement les méthodes antérieures en robustesse.

de.title: R-PGA: Robuste physische adversarische Camouflage-Generierung via wiederbeleuchtbares 3D-Gaussian-Splatting
de.summary: R-PGA entwickelt ein realistisches adversarisches Angriffsrahmenwerk für autonome Fahrsysteme, das Camouflage-Texturen erzeugt, die robust gegen unterschiedliche Beleuchtung, Betrachtungswinkel und Wetterbedingungen sind. Mit fotorealistischem 3D-Rendering und Worst-Case-Szenario-Mining übertrifft der Angriff frühere Methoden in Robustheit erheblich.

es.title: R-PGA: Generación robusta de camuflaje adversario físico mediante Gaussian Splatting 3D relightable
es.summary: R-PGA desarrolla un marco de ataque adversario realista para sistemas de conducción autónoma que genera texturas de camuflaje robustas contra variaciones de iluminación, ángulos de visión y condiciones climáticas. Utilizando renderizado 3D fotorrealista y búsqueda de escenarios del peor caso, el ataque supera significativamente los métodos anteriores en robustez.

title: When Identities Collapse: A Stress-Test Benchmark for Multi-Subject Personalization
summary: A benchmark reveals that current text-to-image models suffer from identity collapse when generating 6-10 subjects, particularly under complex interactions, despite excelling with 2-4 subjects. The study introduces Subject Collapse Rate, a new evaluation metric that better captures these failures than existing metrics.
content:
arXiv:2603.26078v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Subject-driven text-to-image diffusion models have achieved remarkable success in preserving single identities, yet their ability to compose multiple interacting subjects remains largely unexplored and highly challenging. Existing evaluation protocols typically rely on global CLIP metrics, which are insensitive to local identity collapse and fail to capture the severity of multi-subject entanglement. In this paper, we identify a pervasive "Illusion of Scalability" in current models: while they excel at synthesizing 2-4 subjects in simple layouts, they suffer from catastrophic identity collapse when scaled to 6-10 subjects or tasked with complex physical interactions. To systematically expose this failure mode, we construct a rigorous stress-test benchmark comprising 75 prompts distributed across varying subject counts and interaction difficulties (Neutral, Occlusion, Interaction). Furthermore, we demonstrate that standard CLIP-based metrics are fundamentally flawed for this task, as they often assign high scores to semantically correct but identity-collapsed images (e.g., generating generic clones). To address this, we introduce the Subject Collapse Rate (SCR), a novel evaluation metric grounded in DINOv2's structural priors, which strictly penalizes local attention leakage and homogenization. Our extensive evaluation of state-of-the-art models (MOSAIC, XVerse, PSR) reveals a precipitous drop in identity fidelity as scene complexity grows, with SCR approaching 100% at 10 subjects. We trace this collapse to the semantic shortcuts inherent in global attention routing, underscoring the urgent need for explicit physical disentanglement in future generative architectures.

zh.title: 当身份坍塌：多主体个性化的压力测试基准
zh.summary: 一项基准测试表明，当前的文本到图像模型在生成6-10个主体时会出现身份坍塌，特别是在复杂交互下，尽管在2-4个主体时表现出色。该研究引入了主体坍塌率，一个更好地捕捉这些失败的新评估指标，优于现有指标。

fr.title: Quand les Identités s'Effondrent : Un Benchmark de Test de Stress pour la Personnalisation Multi-Sujet
fr.summary: Un benchmark révèle que les modèles texte-image actuels souffrent d'un effondrement d'identité lors de la génération de 6-10 sujets, particulièrement sous des interactions complexes, malgré l'excellence avec 2-4 sujets. L'étude introduit le taux d'effondrement de sujet, une nouvelle métrique d'évaluation qui capture mieux ces échecs que les métriques existantes.

de.title: Wenn Identitäten zusammenbrechen: Ein Stresstest-Benchmark für Multi-Subject-Personalisierung
de.summary: Ein Benchmark zeigt, dass aktuelle Text-zu-Bild-Modelle unter Identitätszusammenbruch leiden, wenn sie 6-10 Subjekte generieren, besonders unter komplexen Interaktionen, trotz hervorragender Leistung bei 2-4 Subjekten. Die Studie führt die Subject-Collapse-Rate ein, eine neue Bewertungsmetrik, die diese Ausfälle besser erfasst als bestehende Metriken.

es.title: Cuando las Identidades se Colapsan: Un Benchmark de Prueba de Estrés para Personalización Multisujetiva
es.summary: Un benchmark revela que los modelos actuales de texto a imagen sufren colapso de identidad al generar 6-10 sujetos, particularmente bajo interacciones complejas, a pesar de destacar con 2-4 sujetos. El estudio introduce la Tasa de Colapso de Sujeto, una nueva métrica de evaluación que captura mejor estos fallos que las métricas existentes.

title: Selective Deficits in LLM Mental Self-Modeling in a Behavior-Based Test of Theory of Mind
summary: Testing leading LLMs on a novel theory-of-mind paradigm shows that while recent models achieve human-level performance at modeling others' mental states, even frontier models fail at self-modeling without reasoning traces. Results suggest LLMs use limited working memory and can engage in strategic deception.
content:
arXiv:2603.26089v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: The ability to represent oneself and others as agents with knowledge, intentions, and belief states that guide their behavior - Theory of Mind - is a human universal that enables us to navigate - and manipulate - the social world. It is supported by our ability to form mental models of ourselves and others. Its ubiquity in human affairs entails that LLMs have seen innumerable examples of it in their training data and therefore may have learned to mimic it, but whether they have actually learned causal models that they can deploy in arbitrary settings is unclear. We therefore develop a novel experimental paradigm that requires that subjects form representations of the mental states of themselves and others and act on them strategically rather than merely describe them. We test a wide range of leading open and closed source LLMs released since 2024, as well as human subjects, on this paradigm. We find that 1) LLMs released before mid-2025 fail at all of our tasks, 2) more recent LLMs achieve human-level performance on modeling the cognitive states of others, and 3) even frontier LLMs fail at our self-modeling task - unless afforded a scratchpad in the form of a reasoning trace. We further demonstrate cognitive load effects on other-modeling tasks, offering suggestive evidence that LLMs are using something akin to limited-capacity working memory to hold these mental representations in mind during a single forward pass. Finally, we explore the mechanisms by which reasoning models succeed at the self- and other-modeling tasks, and show that they readily engage in strategic deception.

zh.title: 基于行为的心智理论测试中LLM心智自我建模的选择性缺陷
zh.summary: 对先进LLM的新型心智理论范式测试表明，虽然最近的模型在建模他人心智状态方面达到了人类水平的性能，但即使是最先进的模型在没有推理迹象的情况下也会在自我建模方面失败。结果表明LLM使用有限的工作记忆，并且可以进行战略欺骗。

fr.title: Déficits Sélectifs dans l'Auto-Modélisation Mentale des LLM dans un Test Comportemental de la Théorie de l'Esprit
fr.summary: Les tests menés sur des LLM de pointe utilisant un nouveau paradigme de théorie de l'esprit montrent que, bien que les modèles récents atteignent une performance au niveau humain pour modéliser les états mentaux d'autrui, même les modèles de pointe échouent à l'auto-modélisation sans traces de raisonnement. Les résultats suggèrent que les LLM utilisent une mémoire de travail limitée et peuvent se livrer à la tromperie stratégique.

de.title: Selektive Mängel in der mentalen Selbstmodellierung von LLMs in einem verhaltensgestützten Test der Theorie des Geistes
de.summary: Tests führender LLMs mit einem neuartigen Paradigma der Theorie des Geistes zeigen, dass aktuelle Modelle beim Modellieren der Geisteszustände anderer Menschen menschliche Leistung erreichen, aber sogar Grenzmodelle bei der Selbstmodellierung ohne Überlegungsspuren fehlschlagen. Die Ergebnisse deuten darauf hin, dass LLMs ein begrenztes Arbeitsgedächtnis nutzen und sich strategischer Täuschung hingeben können.

es.title: Déficits Selectivos en la Automodelización Mental de LLMs en una Prueba Basada en el Comportamiento de la Teoría de la Mente
es.summary: Las pruebas realizadas en LLMs líderes utilizando un nuevo paradigma de teoría de la mente muestran que, aunque los modelos recientes logran un desempeño a nivel humano al modelar los estados mentales de otros, incluso los modelos fronterizos fallan en la automodelización sin rastros de razonamiento. Los resultados sugieren que los LLMs utilizan una memoria de trabajo limitada y pueden participar en engaño estratégico.

title: Dynamic Tokenization via Reinforcement Patching: End-to-end Training and Zero-shot Transfer
summary: ReinPatch introduces the first reinforcement learning framework for jointly optimizing variable-sized sequence patching and downstream models without heuristic rules or continuous relaxations. The method shows strong performance on time-series forecasting and enables extraction of a standalone foundation patcher.
content:
arXiv:2603.26097v1 Announce Type: cross 
Abstract: Efficiently aggregating spatial or temporal horizons to acquire compact representations has become a unifying principle in modern deep learning models, yet learning data-adaptive representations for long-horizon sequence data, especially continuous sequences like time series, remains an open challenge. While fixed-size patching has improved scalability and performance, discovering variable-sized, data-driven patches end-to-end often forces models to rely on soft discretization, specific backbones, or heuristic rules. In this work, we propose Reinforcement Patching (ReinPatch), the first framework to jointly optimize a sequence patching policy and its downstream sequence backbone model using reinforcement learning. By formulating patch boundary placement as a discrete decision process optimized via Group Relative Policy Gradient (GRPG), ReinPatch bypasses the need for continuous relaxations and performs dynamic patching policy optimization in a natural manner. Moreover, our method allows strict enforcement of a desired compression rate, freeing the downstream backbone to scale efficiently, and naturally supports multi-level hierarchical modeling. We evaluate ReinPatch on time-series forecasting datasets, where it demonstrates compelling performance compared to state-of-the-art data-driven patching strategies. Furthermore, our detached design allows the patching module to be extracted as a standalone foundation patcher, providing the community with visual and empirical insights into the segmentation behaviors preferred by a purely performance-driven neural patching strategy.

zh.title: 通过强化补丁的动态分词化：端到端训练和零样本转移
zh.summary: ReinPatch引入了第一个强化学习框架，用于联合优化可变大小序列补丁和下游模型，无需启发式规则或连续松弛。该方法在时间序列预测中表现出色，并能够提取独立的基础补丁器。

fr.title: Tokenisation Dynamique via Patch Renforcé : Entraînement Bout à Bout et Transfert Zero-Shot
fr.summary: ReinPatch introduit le premier cadre d'apprentissage par renforcement pour l'optimisation conjointe des patchs de séquence de taille variable et des modèles en aval sans règles heuristiques ou relaxations continues. La méthode montre des performances solides sur la prévision de séries chronologiques et permet l'extraction d'un patcher fondationnel autonome.

de.title: Dynamische Tokenisierung durch verstärkendes Patching: End-to-End-Training und Zero-Shot-Transfer
de.summary: ReinPatch führt das erste Reinforcement-Learning-Framework ein, das variable Größe Sequenzpatching und nachgelagerte Modelle gemeinsam optimiert, ohne heuristische Regeln oder kontinuierliche Relaxationen. Die Methode zeigt starke Leistung bei der Zeitreihenprognose und ermöglicht die Extraktion eines eigenständigen Foundation-Patchers.

es.title: Tokenización Dinámica mediante Parches de Refuerzo: Entrenamiento Integral y Transferencia Zero-Shot
es.summary: ReinPatch introduce el primer marco de aprendizaje por refuerzo para optimizar conjuntamente parchés de secuencia de tamaño variable y modelos descendentes sin reglas heurísticas ni relajaciones continuas. El método muestra un rendimiento sólido en pronósticos de series de tiempo y permite la extracción de un parchador fundacional independiente.
